name: cpig-render-04-debug-reps-aabb
description: Phase B2 isolation test for BUG-RENDER-DENSE-01. Same mesh-field setup as cpig-render-01 but flips the conduit's _debugReps uniform ON via _PenumbraDebugReps before capture. When _debugReps=1 the shader bypasses rep dispatch and draws every atom as its SOLID AABB box (orange under rep-coloring). PASS verdict (orange box visible) = the marcher reaches the dense-grid atom and the bug is in the rep dispatch / sampler binding / gridSample texture upload (B1b lineage). FAIL verdict (still blank) = the marcher itself doesn't reach the atom (camera framing / scene-AABB / depth state — B1a lineage). Decisive single-PNG discriminator between sub-hypotheses.

setup.vlmDescription: A 3D viewport with a SOLID, FILLED, OPAQUE shaded box (the atom-AABB debug visualization) near the world origin. The box surface must be filled / shaded — colored orange, red, or any solid color — NOT just a transparent wireframe outline with empty interior. The defining feature is that the box's faces are visibly shaded, not see-through. It must NOT be an empty grey viewport, NOT just a thin wireframe box outline, NOT a smooth round ball / sphere, NOT a gyroid lattice.

checkpoints.name: cpig-debug-reps-aabb-render
checkpoints.description: Atom AABB debug render: a solid orange / shaded box at origin (Phase B2 discriminator).
